questionText: The organisation’s governance structure is described in written documents and is known to the staff of the organisation.
guideline:
This governance structure refers to the authority(ies) above the level of the Hospital Manager and may include National/Regional/District levels in the public sector together with the Hospital Board, or corporate structures in the private sector including structures such as Head Office, Regional Office, Hospital Board/Board of Directors/ Governance Committees/any other structures that may exist. 
Please note that full compliance can only be achieved if there is formal evidence of sharing this information with the staff of the organisation. A mere organogram does not render this criterion compliant unless there is a concise description/listing of the key functions of the relevant structures as reflected in this criterion. 
Also take note that some of this information may be contained in Acts, regulations or directives. In the private sector this information may also be published as a corporate document or on the organisation’s website.
Documented evidence is required of a process through which the personnel of the organisation are informed of these responsibilities of the governance structure, e.g. during orientation and induction programmes, staff meetings, information leaflets, memos.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_5-The organisation's governance structure is described in written documents and is known to the staff of the organisation.

questionText: There is an organisational chart or document that describes the lines of authority and accountability between the governance structure and the organisation as well as within the organisation.
guideline: This criterion requires an organisational chart of both the governance structure and the local organisation. These document(s) should also illustrate the relationship between the Hospital Manager and the next level of Governance above him/her. The phrase “lines of authority and accountability” requires more than merely a list of available posts or services rendered; it should be formulated in such a manner that it indicates to each member of staff who his/her direct supervisor is as well as his/her span of responsibility. The names of individuals do not need to be shown. As with any other official document, the organogram should be duly authorised (dated and signed). The titles/post designations of those responsible for governing will automatically be displayed on the organogram provided it is drawn up correctly. This information can also form part of the documentation referred to in 1.1.1.1. Detail about facility managers may also be offered in other documents such as job descriptions, delegations, performance agreements, etc.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7-There is an organisational chart or document that describes the lines of authority and accountability between the governance structure and the organisation as well as within the organisation.

questionText: Those responsible for governance approve and make public the organisation’s mission statement.
guideline:
This section (1.1.1.3 to 10) requires documented evidence of the involvement of the governance structure in the stated activities; 
Please note there is no standardised way in which all the different organisations carry out these functions. Therefore, the official documents in 1.1.1.1, 1.1.1.2 and 1.1.1.4 should guide the assessment of systems and processes in relation to the manner in which these responsibilities are carried out.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_9-Those responsible for governance approve and make public the organisation's mission statement.

questionText: Those responsible for governance approve the managerial policies and plans to operate the organisation.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_11-Those responsible for governance approve the managerial policies and plans to operate the organisation.

questionText: Those responsible for governance approve the budget and allocate resources required to meet the organisation’s mission.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_13-Those responsible for governance approve the budget and allocate resources required to meet the organisation's mission.

questionText: Those responsible for governance appoint the organisation’s senior manager(s) or director(s).
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_15-Those responsible for governance appoint the organisation's senior manager(s) or director(s).

questionText: Those responsible for governance collaborate with the organisation’s managers.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_17-Those responsible for governance collaborate with the organisation's managers.

questionText: Those responsible for governance receive and act upon reports of the quality programme, at least quarterly.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_19-Those responsible for governance receive and act upon reports of the quality programme at least quarterly.

questionText: Those responsible for governance receive and act upon reports on risk management, at least quarterly.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_21-Those responsible for governance receive and act upon reports on risk management at least quarterly.

questionText: Those responsible for governance evaluate the performance of the organisation’s senior manager at least annually.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_23-Those responsible for governance evaluate the performance of the organisation's senior manager at least annually.

questionText: The senior manager manages the day to day operation of the organisation, including those responsibilities described in the position description.
guideline: This criterion is scored PC in situations where the position is temporarily filled (acting capacity).
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_28-The senior manager manages the day-to-day operation of the organisation, including those responsibilities described in the position description.

questionText: The senior manager has the education and experience to match the requirements in the position description.
guideline: Compliance is to be assessed against the job profile/post requirements. Evidence could be found in a copy of the advertisement, interviewing notes or the corporate guideline on the filling of this position.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_30-The senior manager has the education and experience to match the requirements in the position description.

questionText: The senior manager carries out approved policies for management functions.
guideline: This criterion is not assessed on all the policies in the organisation but only those that apply to the senior management level, which includes corporate/national matters, for example. In other words, this criterion is not scored down for deficiencies that derive from the level of individual departments. However, the final rating of this criterion should be in line with the overall level of compliance on the “generic” policies and procedures (SEs 2 to 9).
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_32-The senior manager carries out approved policies for management functions.

questionText: The senior manager assures compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
guideline:
This criterion will be scored compliant by default although a PC rating is given whenever there is actual evidence of non-adherence to any particular legal requirement. In these cases, the transgression needs to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC rating, and it should be based on accurate facts. Common examples of non-conformance with legal requirements include the following deficiencies:
• the required certificates are not available, e.g. fire clearance certificates, electrical installation certificates, commissioning certificates (e.g. Ethylene Oxide sterilisers), pressure test certificates for vessels under pressure
• internal/external financial audits are not conducted
• nurses transcribe doctors’ prescriptions and/or dispense medication
• pharmaceutical items are incorrectly labelled and
• proof of current registration of professional personnel with the relevant Councils is not available.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_34-The senior manager assures compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

questionText: The senior manager responds to any reports from inspecting and regulatory agencies;
guideline:
This requires documented evidence for whatever inspections may have been conducted and depends on national requirement requirements. Examples include inspections by the following authorities:
• the local Fire Authority for fire clearance purposes
• the Department of Radiation Control for radiation safety aspects
• the Department of Labour for health and safety compliance
• the Auditor General’s office (Public Sector) for financial compliance
• the Local Authority to test water supplies and inspect food premises
• the relevant professional bodies where student training is provided and
• licensing authorities for facilities such as the pharmacy, etc.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_36-The senior manager responds to any reports from inspecting and regulatory agencies.

questionText: The senior manager facilitates communication and co-operation between the organisation’s governance structure, management and the community.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_40-The senior manager facilitates communication and cooperation between the organisation's governance structure, management and the community.

questionText: The senior manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources;
guideline: This is assessed against the organisational policy framework and evidence of implementation, and is linked to the next criterion. The criterion score is derived from the final assessment of SE 2, financial management (Section 3.1) and other criteria dealing with adequate supply and effective management of resources (medication, consumables, etc.). The volume and severity of deficiencies in related sections will determine whether both criteria are penalised and to what extent.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_42-The senior manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.

questionText: The senior manager ensures that the required physical facilities, installations and equipment are available and are used optimally to provide the specified services.
guideline: Please note that the term equipment refers to all hospital, as well as medical equipment.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_44-The senior manager ensures that the required physical facilities, installations and equipment are available and are used optimally to provide the specified services.

questionText: The senior manager ensures the implementation of risk management processes and activities.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_46-The senior manager ensures the implementation of risk management processes and activities.

questionText: The senior manager implements processes to monitor patient expectations and satisfaction.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_48-The senior manager implements processes to monitor patient expectations and satisfaction.

questionText: The senior manager implements processes to monitor staff expectations and satisfaction.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_50-The senior manager implements processes to monitor staff expectations and satisfaction.

questionText: The senior manager implements processes for quality management and improvement.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_52-The senior manager implements processes for quality management and improvement.

questionText: The senior manager implements processes to monitor the quality of clinical and other services.
guideline:
This criterion's score should be derived from the ratings obtained for standard 8.2.1, in particular criterion 8.2.1.1. 
The assessment of the latter is in turn influenced by the aggregated scores obtained from the quality improvement standards in the clinical services that require clinical audits and patient record audits to be conducted.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_54-The senior manager implements processes to monitor the quality of clinical and other services.

questionText: The leaders of the organisation are formally identified.
guideline: This information can be obtained from various sources such as the organogram, job descriptions, performance agreements, letters of appointment to committees, designation of leadership roles for various tasks/responsibilities, etc.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_58-The leaders of the organisation are formally identified.

questionText: The leaders work collaboratively to develop and implement the strategic plan of the organisation.
guideline: The term “strategic plan” does not necessarily mean a single document as indicated below. Sometimes organisations refer to “business/operational” plans which may be separate entities or be integrated into the “strategic plans”. In assessing compliance in the public sector, the designated “level” of the hospital and its “service package” as published in national and district documents should be considered. The major private hospital groups publish this information on their websites, brochures, etc. Although these media may not be part of the document referred to as the “strategic plan”, the information does form part of the corporate marketing/public relations strategy, at facility or corporate level.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_60-The leaders work collaboratively to develop and implement the strategic plan of the organisation.

questionText: The organisation’s leaders meet with the leaders of other provider organisations in their community to develop and revise strategic and operational plans to meet the community needs.
guideline: The strategic plan gives rise to the implementation of the mission and the criterion requires not only a documented plan, but also evidence of implementation and this can be assessed by means of progress reports (monthly or quarterly and also annually). Often organisations require each department to compile a departmental “operational plan”. These plans are linked to the organisational strategic/business plan, which is regularly reviewed to reflect the progress made with the implementation of strategic/operational objectives in achieving set targets.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_62-The organisation's leaders meet with the leaders of other provider organisations in their community to develop and revise strategic and operational plans to meet the community needs.

questionText: The organisation has identified its target population.
guideline: The term target population refers to any clearly definable group of individuals and/or families who may experience a need. In health services this is usually confined to those individuals within a particular geographical location and is linked to the population census for that area. Socio-economic categories and disease profiles are considered when planning for healthcare services to be provided.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_66-The organisation has identified its target population.

questionText: The organisation has implemented a communication strategy with these populations.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_68-The organisation has implemented a communication strategy with these populations.

questionText: The organisation provides information on the quality of its services.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_70-The organisation provides information on the quality of its services.

questionText: The organisation’s leaders establish ethical and legal norms that protect patients and their rights.
guideline: The leaders consider national and international ethical norms when developing the organisation’s framework for ethical management. Evidence of compliance exists in the form of documented systems such as a policy framework, guidelines, position statements such as those from professional councils and associations, Acts, regulations, etc. Patient right charters are also relevant to this section. Also note the reference in the intent to the organisation’s marketing activities and the processes in relation to the admission, discharge and transfer of patients. Although this partly refers to formal systems it also requires an organisational system/process/body, e.g. an Ethics Committee or other structure. Final assessment of this criterion is based on evidence of implementation of these principles throughout the organisation and the rating should reflect the aggregated score of relevant standards and criteria.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_74-The organisation's leaders establish ethical and legal norms that protect patients and their rights.

questionText: The organisation discloses its ownership.
guideline: This is generally evident for hospitals in the public sector as well as in major private hospital groups. In some of the “independent hospitals” this information may not be readily available, but could be found in company documents, list of the Board of Directors, etc.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_76-The organisation discloses its ownership.

questionText: The organisation honestly portrays its services to patients.
guideline: There is no single aspect against which compliance is assessed and the criterion is generally rated compliant by default unless there is evidence of a very specific “transgression” such as inadequate/incorrect information to patients (SE 4), complaints about accounts, serious misconduct, financial mismanagement (accounts/billing for services), dishonest/inappropriate marketing of services, etc.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_78-The organisation honestly portrays its services to patients.

questionText: The organisation provides clear admission, treatment, transfer and discharge policies.
guideline: The same general rule applies for “policies and procedures” and the score for this criterion should reflect the findings of the relevant/related criteria in the clinical service elements, including the results obtained from patient record audits. This criterion cannot be marked compliant if there are a large number of non- or partially compliant criteria in the relevant sections in the clinical SEs.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_80-The organisation provides clear admission, treatment, transfer and discharge policies.

questionText: The organisation accurately bills for services.
guideline: This refers to a policy framework and the outcome as recorded in either an internal or external financial audit report. This will be assessed from examination of the complaints register and from the auditors’ reports.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_82-The organisation accurately bills for services.

questionText: The organisation has a committee or other mechanism to control all research undertaken within the organisation.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_84-The organisation has a committee or other mechanism to control all research undertaken within the organisation.

questionText: The organisation’s leaders ensure that policies and procedures guide and support the activities and management of the organisation.
guideline: In measuring this criterion, the same general rules apply as when measuring “implementation of policies and procedures”. This implies that the rating of this criterion should be consistent with the findings on policies and procedures in all the other service elements. This is a “root” criterion and should be scored compliant only if there are no critical/very serious non- or partially compliant criteria, or not many other non- or partially compliant “policies and procedures” criteria in any of the other service elements.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_88-The organisation's leaders ensure that policies and procedures guide and support the activities and management of the organisation.

questionText: A designated manager is responsible for compiling and indexing policies and procedures and for ensuring their circulation, recall and review.
guideline: Evidence is obtained from the written designation/delegation of such responsibility to an individual. The criterion is not prescriptive in terms of whether this individual operates independently or acts as a coordinator of a team or as chairperson of a committee. The criterion should not be scored compliant if there is evidence of non-partial execution of the listed tasks.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_90-A designated manager is responsible for compiling and indexing policies and procedures and for ensuring their circulation, recall and review.

questionText: Policies and procedures are signed by persons authorised to do so.
guideline: This is measured against the documented organisational arrangements regarding such authorisation. Often this information is found in the policy document on “management of policies” or a letter of authorisation/delegation to the respective individuals.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_92-Policies and procedures are signed by persons authorised to do so.

questionText: Policies and procedures are compiled into comprehensive manual(s) or filing system, which is indexed and easily accessible to all personnel.
guideline: This should be interpreted to also mean more than one manual – paper-based or electronic. The effectiveness of the indexing system needs to be tested by random checks on cross references between manuals and policy documents. Accessibility should be tested at departmental level, especially in the case of electronically-based systems.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_94-Policies and procedures are compiled into comprehensive manual(s) or a filing system, which is indexed and easily accessible to all personnel.

questionText: All policies and procedures are reviewed at appropriate intervals, dated and signed.
guideline: The general approach is not to penalise the criterion where there are only a few documents that do not comply. In other words, the general trend should be taken into consideration. It is expected that policies will be reviewed whenever indicated because of changes, but time frames for the routine review of policies should be defined in organisation policy. It is generally accepted that a period of 2 to 3 years between reviews should not be exceeded.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_96-All policies and procedures are reviewed at appropriate intervals, dated and signed.

questionText: There is a mechanism to ensure that policies are known and implemented.
guideline: Compliance can be demonstrated in various ways as organisations have different ways of performing these tasks. Examples of making policies known may include: 1. staff members indicate their acknowledgement by signing on the reverse of each document, on the index or on a separate sheet/form, thus undertaking to keep up to date with all relevant policies; 2. key policies and procedures are discussed at meetings; 3. memos are circulated to inform the staff of new/changed policy matters, etc. The criterion is not prescriptive regarding the method to be used but the system in operation should be tested for effectiveness. Staff members do not necessarily have to receive formal in-service training on policies, but this often happens for important/key aspects.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_98-There is a mechanism to ensure that policies are known and implemented.

questionText: Copies of contracts are made available to those who ensure their implementation.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_102-Copies of contracts are made available to those who ensure their implementation.

questionText: Services provided under contracts/agreements meet patient needs.
guideline: Commonly, this will apply to contracted services for catering, laundry/linen, housekeeping, security, maintenance of buildings, plant and equipment, provisioning services e.g. medication, coal for boilers, etc. It can also apply to providing personnel such as agency nurses, locums, artisans, technicians, etc. If contracts/agreements exist, this criterion is scored compliant by default, unless non-conformance by other service elements indicates a definite shortfall that can justify a NC/PC rating. Where services are rendered without a formal contract/agreement, this will be scored PC.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_104-Services provided under contracts/agreements meet patient needs.

questionText: Contracts and other arrangements are monitored as part of the organisation’s quality management and improvement programme.
guideline: The reference to “the organisation’s quality management and improvement programme” indicates formal monitoring of analysed data. Assessment of this requirement needs to take into consideration that compliance can be achieved in various ways as different organisations may present information in many different ways, e.g. formal monitoring tools/checklists, satisfaction surveys, minutes of meetings, negotiations of contractual arrangements/specifications, service level agreements with service performance indicators, etc. This criterion cannot be scored compliant if 1.2.7.1 is non-compliant (i.e. if copies of the contracts are not available at facility level).
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_106-Contracts and other arrangements are monitored as part of the organisation's quality management and improvement programme.

questionText: There is a mechanism to ensure that all volunteers work under the guidance of suitably qualified health professionals in the employ of the care centre.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_108-There is a mechanism to ensure that all volunteers work under the guidance of suitably qualified health professionals in the employ of the care centre.

questionText: Volunteers sign a memorandum of agreement to abide by the conditions of the centre.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_110-Volunteers sign a memorandum of agreement to abide by the conditions of the centre.

questionText: There are written policies and procedures for the activities of the volunteer service.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_112-There are written policies and procedures for the activities of the volunteer service.

questionText: The organisation’s leaders promote communication among departments, services and individual staff members.
guideline: Consider formal interdepartmental meetings, written communications in the form of policies, memos, service level agreements, clinical audit committees, drug and therapeutic committees, etc.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_116-The organisation's leaders promote communication among departments, services and individual staff members.

questionText: Agendas are prepared for meetings, and the personnel are given timely notification in order to prepare for participation.
guideline: An assessment of a sample of agendas/minutes of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and “general” compliance as the criterion should not be penalised on one or two exceptions. “Timely notification” should be assessed against the organisational policy framework on meetings and the circulation of agendas. This implies that the dates for circulating the agendas should be indicated.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_118-Agendas are prepared for meetings, and the personnel are given timely notification in order to prepare for participation.

questionText: Minutes of meetings are taken and are circulated to all relevant personnel.
guideline: An assessment of a sample of minutes of meetings should be undertaken to determine the trend and “general” compliance as the criterion should not be penalised for one or two exceptions. The manner in which minutes are circulated should be assessed against the organisational policy framework and should consist of documented evidence of the circulation and acknowledgment of receipt.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_120-Minutes of meetings are taken and are circulated to all relevant personnel.

questionText: There is a mechanism to ensure that key issues resulting from meetings of the governing body and/or the management of the organisation are communicated to and acted upon by the personnel.
guideline: The existing “mechanism” should be assessed for effectiveness in conveying this information to the personnel but please note the importance of searching for evidence of “and acted upon”. This generally requires that minutes should reflect the allocation of responsibilities for carrying out tasks and that these are reported on at subsequent meetings. Often feedback from the governing body meeting is via written internal communication (memo) but if it is stated that it is done at an “open” staff meetings, there should be documented evidence that this does actually happen.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_122-There is a mechanism to ensure that key issues resulting from meetings of the governing body and/or the management of the organisation are communicated to and acted upon by the personnel.

questionText: The organisation ensures that a qualified individual manages each department or service in the organisation.
guideline: National regulations and guidelines will apply. The qualifications of the incumbent will be assessed against the stated requirements for the position. The organogram should indicate the departmental arrangements and compliance is to be assessed against job profiles/post requirements. Besides departments, the “generic” services such as patient rights, information management, health and safety, infection control, quality management, resuscitation, medical equipment management, etc. are included. The final assessment score will be determined by findings from all departments/services. For example, this criterion may be scored PC: 1. If individual critical posts e.g. nursing manager, medical manager, pharmacy manager etc. are vacant; 2. If there is a high volume of departmental/service heads vacancies; 3. If there is a large number of departmental heads in acting positions.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_127-The organisation ensures that a qualified individual manages each department or service in the organisation.

questionText: The responsibilities of each departmental manager are defined in writing.
guideline: As for 1.3.1.1 with special reference to key performance areas. The final assessment score will be determined by aggregated findings from all departments/services and relevant criteria in SE 2. Refer to the comment under 1.3.1.3 with regard to “generic” functions.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_129-The responsibilities of each departmental manager are defined in writing.

questionText: The departmental or service manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.
guideline: The score of this criterion should be based on the aggregated scores from the individual services and should take into consideration any critical/serious/important shortfalls identified with regard to, e.g. physical structure, maintenance installations such as vacuum, oxygen and water, hand washing facilities, etc. that may have an negative impact on service delivery, patient care, and the safety of patients, staff and visitors.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_131-The departmental or service manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.

questionText: The departmental or service manager ensures that there are sufficient personnel to provide the services.
guideline: The score of this criterion is influenced by the outcome of the assessment of relevant criteria during the departmental surveys. All categories of staff in all departments (clinical and non-clinical) are included. Take note of links with relevant criteria in SE 2, especially standard 2.1.1. The score of this criterion should be based on the aggregated scores from the individual services and should take into consideration any critical/serious/important shortfalls identified with regard to staff shortages (both numbers and skills) that impact negatively on patient care and safety. A NC or PC rating for this criterion should be supported by staff numbers (vacancies) as listed in the accompanying “Situational Analysis” document.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_133-The departmental or service manager ensures that there are sufficient personnel to provide the services.

questionText: The departmental or service manager ensures that resources are available to provide those services
guideline: The score of this criterion should be based on the aggregated scores of relevant criteria in individual services and should take into consideration any critical/serious/important shortfalls identified with regard to consumables, medication etc. that impact negatively on patient care and service delivery. These items exclude physical resources such as facilities, installations and equipment, which are dealt with in criterion 1.2.2.3.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_135-The departmental or service manager ensures that resources are available to provide those services.

questionText: Departmental or service managers provide orientation and training for all personnel of the department or service appropriate to their responsibilities.
guideline: Documented evidence if required of orientation/induction/in-service training across the entire organisation, and a NC/PC rating is justified where there are any critical/major/serious shortfalls with regard to training (note any critical deficiencies in relation to resuscitation, health & safety, infection control, pain management), or a general finding/high volume/trend of non-/partially compliant criteria in the service elements.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_137-Departmental or service managers provide orientation and training for all personnel of the department or service appropriate to their responsibilities.

questionText: There is coordination and integration of services with other departments and services.
guideline: This criterion's score should be a culmination of the scores obtained at departmental levels throughout the organisation. The results obtained from patient record audits of aspects pertaining to continuity of care and interdepartmental Coordination will also influence this criterion score.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_139-There is coordination and integration of services with other departments and services.

questionText: Departmental managers implement quality control and improvement programmes.
guideline: This criterion's score should be a culmination of the scores obtained at departmental levels throughout the organisation. Also take note of the relevant links with Service Element 8.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_141-Departmental managers implement quality control and improvement programmes.

questionText: Care planning and delivery is integrated and co-ordinated among care settings, departments and services.
guideline: The score of this criterion will be determined by the aggregated ratings of the clinical criteria that deal with the implementation of policies/procedures/protocols/guidelines i.e. the results of clinical audits. Also consider the results of patient record audits/clinical audits as these reflect the level of implementation of relevant policies on clinical care.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_145-Care planning and delivery is integrated and co-ordinated among care settings, departments and services.

questionText: Clinical practice guidelines relevant to the patients and services of the organisation are implemented to guide uniform patient care processes.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_147-Clinical practice guidelines relevant to the patients and services of the organisation are implemented to guide uniform patient care processes.

questionText: The hospital maintains a clinical record for each patient.
guideline: This may be a paper based or electronic record. Patient held records for outpatients will be scored PC because the record, with its relevant clinical information, is not maintained by the hospital.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_149-The hospital maintains a clinical record for each patient.

questionText: The patients' clinical records are completed according to guidelines determined by the organisation.
guideline: This refers to the relevant forms/documents that need to be present in the patient folder, as well as the completeness of entries made in the record. For this purpose the results of patient record audits need to be taken into account. Therefore the score of this criterion needs to reflect the aggregated average score of all linked criteria.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_151-The patients' clinical records are completed according to guidelines determined by the organisation.

questionText: A designated individual is responsible for providing support for the organisation’s human resource strategy.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_157-A designated individual is responsible for providing support for the organisation's human resource strategy.

questionText: The human resource manager is suitably qualified and experienced in human resource management.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_159-The human resource manager is suitably qualified and experienced in human resource management.

questionText: The human resource manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel and that they are implemented.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_161-The human resource manager ensures that policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel and that they are implemented.

questionText: The human resource manager uses information on staffing needs provided by clinical and managerial personnel to ensure adequate staff provision.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_163-The human resource manager uses information on staffing needs provided by clinical and managerial personnel to ensure adequate staff provision.

questionText: Details of the organisation’s absenteeism, sickness rates and staff turnover rates are recorded and analysed, to allow for informed decision making by the management of the organisation.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_165-Details of the organisation's absenteeism, sickness rates and staff turnover rates are recorded and analysed, to allow for informed decision making by the management of the organisation.

questionText: Details of the staff establishment (i.e. available posts, filled and vacant posts) are recorded and analysed to allow for informed decision making by the organisation’s management.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_167-Details of the staff establishment (i.e. available posts, filled and vacant posts) are recorded and analysed to allow for informed decision making by the organisation's management.

questionText: Receptionists, telephonists, clerical support personnel and porters are allocated to wards and departments in accordance with their needs.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_169-Receptionists, telephonists, clerical support personnel and porters are allocated to wards and departments in accordance with their needs.

questionText: A designated member of staff is responsible for the storage and retrieval of personnel records.
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_173-A designated member of staff is responsible for the storage and retrieval of personnel records.

questionText: There is documented personnel information on each staff member.

questionText: Personnel files are standardised.

dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6893-Information exchanged includes the patient's progress.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6895-The author can be identified for each patient record entry.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6897-The date and time of each patient record entry can be identified.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6902-Written policies and procedures for assessing patients and their families on admission and during ongoing care are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6904-Only those individuals permitted by applicable laws, regulations and professional registration perform the assessments.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6906-The scope and content of assessment by each discipline is defined.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6908-Assessments are performed within appropriate time frames and are comprehensively documented in the patient's records according to organisation policy.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6910-The date and time of assessment can be identified.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6914-Each patient admitted has an initial assessment which meets organisation policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6916-The initial assessment includes a health history.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6918-The initial assessment includes a physical examination.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6920-The initial assessment includes a nutritional assessment of the adolescent.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6922-The initial assessment includes a psychological and behavioural assessment of the adolescent.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6924-The initial assessment includes a developmental history and current functional assessment of the adolescent.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6926-The initial assessment includes a social, cultural and where applicable, economic assessment of the adolescent and his/her family.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6928-The initial assessment includes a genogram of family construction and functional assessment of the family.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6930-The initial assessment results in an understanding of all previous care and the care that the adolescent and his/her family is presently seeking.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6932-The initial assessment results in a diagnosis and the formulation of an individualised plan of care for the patient.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6934-The organisation identifies patients in pain during the initial assessment process.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6936-The organisation identifies those patient populations and special situations for which the initial assessment process is modified.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6938-Those special patient populations receive individualised assessments.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6940-The findings of assessments performed outside the organisation are verified on admission.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6942-Any significant changes in the patient's condition since the assessment performed outside of the organisation are noted in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6944-A process is in place to identify needs for discharge planning at the initial assessment.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6948-Assessment findings are documented in the patient's record and are readily available to those responsible for the patient's care.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6950-Patient assessment data and information are analysed and integrated by those responsible for the patient's care.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6952-Patient needs are prioritised based on assessment results.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6954-The patient and/or his or her family participate in the decisions regarding the priority needs to be met.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6959-Patient and personnel accommodation in the service is adequate to meet patient care needs.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6961-There is evidence that equipment is maintained in accordance with the policies of the organisation.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6963-Resuscitation equipment is available in accordance with the policies of the organisation and includes a) - m) in the standard intent above.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6965-Each patient has access to a nurse call system at all times.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6967-Oxygen and vacuum supplies meet patient care requirements.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6969-When patients receive oxygen from a cylinder, the cylinder pressures are monitored according to organisation policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6971-Electricity and water is available in accordance with the policies and arrangements of the organisation.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6975-The care for each patient is planned, provided and noted in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6977-The patient's response to care and therapeutic interventions is documented in the patient record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6979-Meetings to discuss patient care/treatment regimes are held by the multidisciplinary team at regular intervals as determined by organisational policy and documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6981-All procedures and diagnostic tests ordered and performed, are documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6983-The results of procedures and diagnostic tests performed are available in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6985-Orders are found in a uniform location in patient records.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6987-Only those permitted to write orders do so.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6989-Reassessments are performed at appropriate, regular intervals and following a change in the patient's condition, and documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6991-Care plans are revised when necessary in response to the findings of reassessments.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6995-There is evidence of regular psychotherapeutic interviews as indicated by the programme and individual patient's needs.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6997-There is documented participation in a structured therapeutic programme six to seven hours per day.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_6999-There is a range of therapeutic activities available, according to the identified needs of the patient.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7001-There is documented participation of the patient with his or her family or significant other(s) in group therapy, as appropriate.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7003-The patient has the least restrictive environment possible, with any restrictions placed upon him/her written into the treatment plan.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7005-Implemented policies and procedures guide the freedom of movement within the facility and weekend or other leave from the organisation.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7009-The assessment process makes provision for patients in pain to be identified.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7011-Patients in pain receive care according to pain management guidelines.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7013-There is evidence that the effectiveness of pain and symptom management is monitored.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7015-Patients and families are educated about pain and pain management.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7017-The organisation has processes to educate health professionals in assessing and managing pain.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7019-Personnel provide respectful and compassionate care to dying patients.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7023-Documented policies, clinical guidelines or standard operating procedures for identified high risk patients and procedures, which include items (a)-(h) in the standard intent above as a minimum, are available and re

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7025-In-service training is provided to personnel to ensure they understand the intent and content of the policies and procedures.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7027-Patients receive care consistent with the policies and procedures.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7031-There is a documented process for the obtaining of informed consent.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7033-Consent forms are completed comprehensively and available in the patient records.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7035-Verbal consent is obtained and recorded according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7037-Patients and their families or decision-makers receive adequate information in a clear and understandable way to enable them to participate in care decisions.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7039-There is a written policy guiding the consent for HIV testing which is specific to patients in mental health care organisations.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7041-There is a documented process for obtaining informed consent for the transfusion of blood and blood products.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7046-Medication is ordered according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7048-All storage areas for medication and pharmaceutical supplies comply with current pharmaceutical acts and regulations and manufacturer guidelines (for example, security, temperature, light and humidity control).

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7050-Medication is stored in a locked storage device or cabinet that is accessible only to authorised personnel.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7052-Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisational policy is stored in a cabinet of substantial construction, for which only authorised personnel have the keys.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7054-Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisation policy is accurately accounted for.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7056-Medication is securely and legibly labelled with relevant information as required by legislation and organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7058-Medication is stored in a clean environment.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7060-A dedicated refrigerator is available for medication requiring storage at low temperatures.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7062-The temperature of the refrigerator is monitored and recorded according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7064-Appropriate action is taken and recorded when the temperature of the refrigerator is outside the recommended range.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7066-Expiry dates (including those of emergency medication) are checked regularly at defined intervals according to organisational policy and medication is replaced before the expiry date.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7070-Policies and procedures that guide the safe prescribing and administration of medication are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7072-Only those permitted by the organisation and by relevant laws and regulations prescribe medication.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7074-Only those permitted by the organisation and by relevant laws and regulations administer medication.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7076-There is a process to place limits, when appropriate, on the prescribing or ordering practices of individuals.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7078-Relevant personnel are trained in correct prescribing, ordering and administration practices.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7080-On admission, all current medication taken by the patient is documented in the patient record, including herbal and over-the-counter medication.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7082-Verbal and telephonic medication prescriptions are documented according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7084-There is evidence that patients are identified before medication is administered.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7086-Medication is checked against the original prescriptions and administered as prescribed.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7088-The medication prescribed for and administered to each patient is recorded.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7090-Healthcare professionals monitor medication effects on patients collaboratively.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7092-Therapeutic plasma medication concentrations are checked in accordance with manufacturers' guidelines and organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7094-Adverse drug reactions (ADR) are observed, recorded and reported through a process and within a time frame defined by the organisation.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7096-Medication errors are reported through a process and within a time frame defined by the organisation.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7101-Food appropriate to the patient is regularly available.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7103-Food orders appropriate for the patient's nutritional status and needs are documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7105-Wherever possible, patient food preferences are respected and substitutions made available.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7107-Patient food preferences consider cultural and religious requirements.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7109-When families provide food, they are educated about the patient's dietary limitations.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7111-Patients assessed as being at nutritional risk receive nutrition therapy.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7113-A collaborative process is used to plan, deliver and monitor nutrition therapy.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7115-Nutrition therapy provided, whether oral, enteral or parenteral, is documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7117-Response to nutrition therapy is monitored and recorded.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7122-Patients and families' educational needs are assessed and recorded.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7124-There is a uniform process for the recording of patient education information.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7126-Patients and families are educated about participation in the care process.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7128-Patients and families are informed about the financial implications of care decisions.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7130-Patients and their families (when required) are educated about their diagnosis and relevant health risks.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7132-The organisation identifies and establishes relationships with community resources that support continuing mental and physical health promotion and disease prevention education.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7134-Patients and families are referred to these organisations as appropriate.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7138-The patient and family are taught in a language and format that they can understand.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7140-Those who provide education have the knowledge and communication skills for effective education.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7142-Health professionals caring for the patient work collaboratively when appropriate.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7144-Interaction between personnel, the patient and family is noted in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7149-Policies and procedures that guide the movement of patients within the organisation are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7151-Individuals responsible for the patient's care and its coordination are identified for all phases of patient care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7153-Continuity and coordination are evident throughout all phases of patient care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7155-When a patient is transferred within the organisation, they are accompanied by their patient record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7157-Patient handover between healthcare professionals is standardised according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7161-Policies and procedures that guide the movement of patients for referral to another organisation are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7163-Patients are accompanied and monitored by an appropriately qualified person during transfer.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7165-A copy of the referral note is available in the patient record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7167-Follow-up care based on the findings of investigations and/or consultations performed outside the referring organisation is documented in the patient record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7171-There is a documented process for transferring patients to other organisations.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7173-When a patient is transferred to another organisation, the receiving organisation is given a written summary of the patient's clinical condition and the interventions performed by the referring organisation.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7175-A copy of the transfer summary is available in the patient record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7177-The transferring organisation documents the transfer in the organisation's patient record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7179-The referring organisation determines that the receiving organisation can meet the patient's continuing care needs, and establishes arrangements to ensure continuity.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7181-Patients are accompanied and monitored by an appropriately qualified person during transfer.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7185-There is a documented process to discharge patients.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7187-Where the initial assessment indicates that discharge planning will be required, planning requirements are included in the patient's care plan and completed as scheduled.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7189-The organisation works with the family, caregivers, healthcare practitioners and agencies outside the organisation to ensure timely and appropriate discharge and continuity of care.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7191-Patients and where appropriate their families or caregivers are given understandable follow-up instructions which are documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7193-A discharge summary, which includes (a)-(g) in the standard intent above as a minimum, is written by the medical practitioner when each patient is discharged.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7195-Each record contains a copy of the discharge summary.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7197-When appropriate the patient is given a copy of the discharge summary.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7202-There is a written quality improvement programme for the adolescent service that is developed and agreed upon by the personnel of the service.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7204-Indicators of performance are identified to evaluate the quality of treatment and patient care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7206-Processes are selected in order of priority for evaluation and improvement in the quality of treatment and care.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7208-The quality improvement cycle includes the monitoring and evaluation of the standards set, and remedial action implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7210-A documentation audit system is in place.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7212-An incident managing system for monitoring near misses/adverse events/sentinel events is implemented, which includes the documentation of responses to recorded incidents and interventions to prevent recurrence of th

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7217-There are processes that support patient and family rights during care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7219-There are processes to ensure that care is respectful of the patient's personal values and beliefs.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7221-Measures are taken to protect the patient's privacy, person and possessions.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7223-Personnel respect the rights of patients and families to treatment and to refuse.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7225-Patients are informed of their right not to donate human tissue or to participate in research.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7230-There is a programme, which is implemented, to reduce the risks of healthcare associated infections in patients and healthcare workers.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7232-The department participates in the overall programme for quality management and improvement of infection control.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7234-The department identifies the procedures and processes associated with the risk of infection, and implements strategies to reduce risk.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7236-Personnel are trained in correct hand washing procedures.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7238-Organisational policy on handling, storage and disposal of healthcare waste is implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7240-Individuals who collect specimens are trained in the proper collection and handling of microbiological specimens.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7242-The department provides education on infection control practices to personnel, doctors, patients and, as appropriate, family and other caregivers.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7247-The department conducts ongoing monitoring of risks through documented assessments as part of organisation's risk management processes.

questionText: Severity: Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7249-The Health and Safety representative for the department supervises implementation of the organisation's health and safety programme.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7251-Fire safety measures are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7253-Relevant personnel are trained in the procedures relating to the reporting and investigation of near misses/adverse events/sentinel events.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7255-Security measures are implemented to ensure the safety of patients, personnel and visitors.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7257-Where vulnerable patients are cared for, special safety and security measures are implemented in accordance with organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_7259-All personnel are trained regarding their role in providing a safe and secure patient care facility.

dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_1002-A senior professional who is suitably qualified and experienced oversees the electro-convulsive therapy (ECT) service.
