dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8131-Reassessments are performed at appropriate, regular intervals and following a change in the patient's condition, and documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8133-Care plans are revised when necessary in response to the findings of reassessments.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8135-All mentally handicapped patients have a physical and dental examination done at least annually.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8137-There is evidence of regular psychotherapeutic interviews as indicated by the programme and individual patient's needs.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8139-There is documented participation in a structured therapeutic programme six to seven hours per day.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8141-There is a range of therapeutic activities available, according to the identified needs of the patient.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8143-There is documented participation of the patient with his or her family or significant other(s) in group therapy, as appropriate.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8145-The patient has the least restrictive environment possible, with any restrictions placed upon him/her written into the treatment plan.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8147-The assessment process makes provision for patients in pain to be identified.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8149-Patients in pain receive care according to pain management guidelines.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8151-There is evidence that the effectiveness of pain and symptom management is monitored.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8153-Patients and families are educated about pain and pain management.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8155-The organisation has processes to educate health professionals in assessing and managing pain.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8157-Personnel provide respectful and compassionate care to dying patients.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8159-Documented policies, clinical guidelines or standard operating procedures for identified high risk patients and procedures, which include items (a)-(g) in the standard intent above as a minimum, are available and re

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8161-In-service training is provided to personnel to ensure they understand the intent and content of the policies and procedures.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8163-Patients receive care consistent with the policies and procedures.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8165-There is a documented process for the obtaining of informed consent.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8167-Consent forms are completed comprehensively and available in the patient records.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8169-Verbal consent is obtained and recorded according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8171-Patients and their families or decision-makers receive adequate information in a clear and understandable way to enable them to participate in care decisions.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8173-There is a written policy guiding the consent for HIV testing which is specific to patients in mental health care organisations.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8175-There is a documented process for obtaining informed consent for the transfusion of blood and blood products.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8178-Medication is ordered according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8180-All storage areas for medication and pharmaceutical supplies comply with current pharmaceutical acts and regulations and manufacturer guidelines (for example, security, temperature, light and humidity control).

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8182-Medication is stored in a locked storage device or cabinet that is accessible only to authorised personnel.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8184-Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisational policy is stored in a cabinet of substantial construction, for which only authorised personnel have the keys.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8186-Medication identified for special control by legislation or organisation policy is accurately accounted for.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8188-Medication is securely and legibly labelled with relevant information as required by legislation and organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8190-Medication is stored in a clean environment.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8192-A dedicated refrigerator is available for medication requiring storage at low temperatures.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8194-The temperature of the refrigerator is monitored and recorded according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8196-Appropriate action is taken and recorded when the temperature of the refrigerator is outside the recommended range.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8198-Expiry dates (including those of emergency medication) are checked regularly at defined intervals according to organisational policy and medication is replaced before the expiry date.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8200-Policies and procedures that guide the safe prescribing and administration of medication are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8202-Only those permitted by the organisation and by relevant laws and regulations prescribe medication.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8204-Only those permitted by the organisation and by relevant laws and regulations administer medication.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8206-There is a process to place limits, when appropriate, on the prescribing or ordering practices of individuals.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8208-Relevant personnel are trained in correct prescribing, ordering and administration practices.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8210-On admission, all current medication taken by the patient is documented in the patient record, including herbal and over-the-counter medication.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8212-Verbal and telephonic medication prescriptions are documented according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8214-There is evidence that patients are identified before medication is administered.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8216-Medication is checked against the original prescriptions and administered as prescribed.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8218-The medication prescribed for and administered to each patient is recorded.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8220-Healthcare professionals monitor medication effects on patients collaboratively.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8222-Therapeutic plasma medication concentrations are checked in accordance with manufacturers' guidelines and organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8224-Adverse drug reactions (ADR) are observed, recorded and reported through a process and within a time frame defined by the organisation.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8226-Medication errors are reported through a process and within a time frame defined by the organisation.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8229-Food appropriate to the patient is regularly available.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8231-Food orders appropriate for the patient's nutritional status and needs are documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8233-Wherever possible, patient food preferences are respected and substitutions made available.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8235-Patient food preferences consider cultural and religious requirements.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8237-When families provide food, they are educated about the patient's dietary limitations.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8239-Patients assessed as being at nutritional risk receive nutrition therapy.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8241-A collaborative process is used to plan, deliver and monitor nutrition therapy.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8243-Nutrition therapy provided, whether oral, enteral or parenteral, is documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8245-Response to nutrition therapy is monitored and recorded.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8248-Patients and families' educational needs are assessed and recorded.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8250-There is a uniform process for the recording of patient education information.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8252-Patients and families are educated about participation in the care process.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8254-Patients and families are informed about the financial implications of care decisions.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8256-Patients and their families are educated about their diagnosis and relevant health risks.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8258-The organisation identifies and establishes relationships with community resources that support continuing mental and physical health promotion and disease prevention education.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8260-Patients and families are referred to these organisations as appropriate.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8262-The patient and family are taught in a language and format that they can understand.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8264-Those who provide education have the knowledge and communication skills for effective education.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8266-Health professionals caring for the patient work collaboratively when appropriate.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8268-Interaction between personnel, the patient and family is noted in the patient's record.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8271-Policies and procedures that guide the movement of patients within the organisation are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8273-Individuals responsible for the patient's care and its coordination are identified for all phases of patient care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8275-Continuity and coordination are evident throughout all phases of patient care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8277-When a patient is transferred within the organisation, they are accompanied by their patient record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8279-Patient handover between healthcare professionals is standardised according to organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8281-Policies and procedures that guide the movement of patients for referral to another organisation are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8283-Patients are accompanied and monitored by an appropriately qualified person during transfer.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8285-A copy of the referral note is available in the patient record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8287-Follow-up care based on the findings of investigations and/or consultations performed outside the referring organisation is documented in the patient record.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8289-There is a documented process for transferring patients to other organisations.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8291-When a patient is transferred to another organisation, the receiving organisation is given a written summary of the patient's clinical condition and the interventions performed by the referring organisation.

questionText: CRITICAL Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8293-A copy of the transfer summary is available in the patient record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8295-The transferring organisation documents the transfer in the organisation's patient record.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8297-The referring organisation determines that the receiving organisation can meet the patient's continuing care needs, and establishes arrangements to ensure continuity.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8299-Patients are accompanied and monitored by an appropriately qualified person during transfer.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8301-There is a documented process to discharge patients.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8303-Where the initial assessment indicates that discharge planning will be required, planning requirements are included in the patient's care plan and completed as scheduled.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8305-The organisation works with the family, caregivers, healthcare practitioners and agencies outside the organisation to ensure timely and appropriate discharge and continuity of care.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8307-Patients and where appropriate their families or caregivers are given understandable follow-up instructions which are documented in the patient's record.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8309-A discharge summary, which includes (a)-(g) in the standard intent above as a minimum, is written by the medical practitioner when each patient is discharged.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8311-Each record contains a copy of the discharge summary.

questionText: Severity: Moderate
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8313-When appropriate the patient is given a copy of the discharge summary.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8316-There is a written quality improvement programme for the mentally handicapped service that is developed and agreed upon by the personnel of the service.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8318-Indicators of performance are identified to evaluate the quality of treatment and patient care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8320-Processes are selected in order of priority for evaluation and improvement in the quality of treatment and care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8322-The quality improvement cycle includes the monitoring and evaluation of the standards set, and remedial action implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8324-A documentation audit system is in place.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8326-An incident managing system for monitoring near misses/adverse events/sentinel events is implemented, which includes the documentation of responses to recorded incidents and interventions to prevent recurrence of th

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8329-There are processes that support patient and family rights during care.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8331-There are processes to ensure that care is respectful of the patient's personal values and beliefs.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8333-Measures are taken to protect the patient's privacy, person and possessions.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8335-Personnel respect the rights of patients and families to treatment and to refuse treatment.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8337-Patients are informed of their right not to donate human tissue or to participate in research.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8340-There is a programme, which is implemented, to reduce the risks of healthcare associated infections in patients and healthcare workers.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8342-The department participates in the overall programme for quality management and improvement of infection control.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8344-The department identifies the procedures and processes associated with the risk of infection, and implements strategies to reduce risk.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8346-Personnel are trained in correct hand washing procedures.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8348-Organisational policy on handling, storage and disposal of healthcare waste is implemented.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8350-Individuals who collect specimens are trained in the proper collection and handling of microbiological specimens.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8352-The department provides education on infection control practices to personnel, doctors, patients and, as appropriate, family and other caregivers.

questionText: ROOT Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8355-The department conducts ongoing monitoring of risks through documented assessments as part of organisation's risk management processes.

questionText: Severity: Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8357-The Health and Safety representative for the department supervises implementation of the organisation's health and safety programme.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8359-Fire safety measures are implemented.

questionText: Severity: Serious
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8361-Relevant personnel are trained in the procedures relating to the reporting and investigation of near misses/adverse events/sentinel events.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8363-Security measures are implemented to ensure the safety of patients, personnel and visitors.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8365-Where vulnerable patients are cared for, special safety and security measures are implemented in accordance with organisational policy.

questionText: Severity: Very Serious Category: Pat & Staff Safety
dhis2DataElementName: SURV_HOSP_8367-All personnel are trained regarding their role in providing a safe and secure patient care facility.
